messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "oem-cell-double-squeeze-foreclosure",
 "title": "The 0.22 OEM/integrator cell is squeezed from both sides — branded incumbents win the spec, Taiwanese ODMs win the white-label — leaving no seat for a CE entrant",
 "narrative": "cust.oem carries 0.22 of the buyer mix with no derivation, no evidence rows, and needs-source maturity — yet it silently props up 22% of the SAM as a fallback channel if large operators prove hard. The corpus says the cell is real but not contestable: every integrator case study found shows integrators selecting branded incumbents on certification, DCIM compatibility and support, while the actual white-label volume is already served by established OEM/ODM specialists (Digipower-class manufacturers) competing on Asian cost structures. A new CE industrial entrant is neither the trusted brand nor the cost-floor ODM. This is not a smaller cell — it is a cell that exists for other players, which is why the correct model operation is exclusion, not shrinkage.",
 "category": "boundary",
 "targetNodes": [
  "cust.oem"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Rack integrators and distributors source PDUs either as branded components (feature/cert/DCIM-driven) or as private-label from dedicated ODM houses whose entire business is OEM/ODM design at scale",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c13",
    "digest:cust-mix#c14"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "A pre-launch CE entrant fails both selection filters simultaneously: no installed-base/DCIM ecosystem to win the branded path, no volume cost position to win the white-label path — and no source quantifies any historical case of a new regional entrant capturing integrator share",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c14",
    "ledger:cust.oem"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The 0.22 cell should be treated as foreclosed for the Year-1 horizon; SAM and the YAM pipeline built on four contestable channels actually rest on at most three, concentrating execution risk on the already-fragile large-operator cell",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.oem",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The OEM channel reads as a diversification hedge — 'if direct is slow, we white-label.' Qualitative documentation that the channel exists (buyer's guides listing OEM/private-label options) masquerades as evidence that it is open; the founder's risk list checks 'multiple channels' without asking who actually wins each one.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "Two CE rack integrators or master distributors confirming open white-label PDU sourcing rounds with a realistic qualification path for a new regional manufacturer at viable margins.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.35,
  "rationale": "Integrator preference for branded incumbents is consistently documented across the retrieved case studies, and the ODM cost squeeze is structural; but the cell is entirely unquantified, and a differentiated intelligent-PDU feature set could crack specific integrators — foreclosure is likely partial rather than total.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "cust.oem",
   "op": "exclude",
   "value": null,
   "note": "Cell treated as unavailable to this entrant in the modeled window — foreclosure, not shrinkage; distinct from lowering the share"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Brand composition of PDUs in CE integrator rack-build catalogs and reference designs",
   "where": "Rittal/nVent/regional integrator configurators and partner catalogs; Vertiv and Legrand partner-program case studies",
   "threshold": "Zero non-top-5 PDU brands appearing in new integrator reference builds over two quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "ODM expansion into Europe (local stock, CE/TÜV-certified white-label lines)",
   "where": "Digipower and peer ODM product/price lists; distributor line-card announcements (e.g., master distributors adding Asian PDU lines)",
   "threshold": "≥1 major CE distributor adding an ODM white-label PDU line",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Test the cell directly: solicit white-label RFQ terms from 3 CE integrators/distributors to establish real qualification requirements and margin floors",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "cust.oem",
   "note": "Converts an evidence-free 0.22 into a priced, dated go/no-go on the channel"
  },
  {
   "action": "If foreclosed, rebalance the funnel to mid/enterprise direct plus distributor pull-through, and re-cost the Year-1 plan against a 3-channel SAM",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Better to shrink the plan deliberately than discover the phantom channel in Q3"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "ledger-self-audit",
 "riskId": "risk.ledger-self-audit.oem-cell-double-squeeze-foreclosure"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
